title: metastelma palustre
intro: Metastelma palustre is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the Marsh Milkweed Vine or Swamp Metastelma. It is native to the wetlands of tropical Africa, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Herbal preparations of Metastelma palustre can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
